extra_model_fields.notes: Changes are additive and focused on documentation/auditability. Risk is limited to wording/formula text updates; verify formulas are not accidentally converted to values during edits.
Changes are additive and focused on documentation/auditability. Risk is limited to wording/formula text updates; verify formulas are not accidentally converted to values during edits.

planned_changes: Open tailings_trigger_review.xlsx and add a new sheet named 'Formula_Audit'. Populate it with rows mapping key cell addresses to their exact Excel formula text (include Packet_Input refs, Piezometer_Review Delta/Status/Weeks formulas, projection cells I:K formulas, Action_Analysis formulas, and Decision_Table links).
Generate a plaintext export [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] containing the same cell->formula mapping for toolless review and attach it to the attempt artifacts directory.

Search for any wording or trace-derived notes referencing the pause action; explicitly state in both the workbook (cell comment or a dedicated assumptions sheet) and deposition_decision_memo.md that 'pause' means: 'sets projected rise = 0.4 * original rise (i.e., 40% of original)' (or, if intended differently, update formulas and wording to match the intended interpretation).

Update deposition_decision_memo.md to include a compact scenario-summary table (columns: Scenario, Parameter change, Key outputs [P1/P2/P3 delta or weeks-to-trigger], Resulting action label) covering baseline, worse-case (+10% rise), and better-case (-10% rise) sensitivity checks.
Add a small 'VERSION' and 'VALIDATION' block in the workbook (e.g., cell on a sheet) and a one-line verification statement in the memo that projection cells contain formulas (not hard-coded values).

Re-run the deterministic precheck and include any updated grader/verifier metadata in the revised submission. Confirm that [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] is present so graders can validate formulas without requiring openpyxl.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve auditability and remove minor ambiguity flagged by grader/verifier: include explicit formula text export, clarify pause-effect wording, and make sensitivity outcomes immediately visible in the memo.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission satisfies the deliverable contract and scores highly. Two minor issues were identified: (1) programmatic inspection of the .xlsx formulas was not possible in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), leaving a small residual auditability uncertainty; (2) a minor wording ambiguity about the numeric effect of the 'pause' action (trace shows 'pause action=60%' vs memo 'reduces rise to 40%'). Both are minor and do not change the recommended operational decision, but addressing them will improve reproducibility and auditability.
